reviewer_copy_rule: Give one clean copy to each genuinely independent reviewer; do not merge, pre-segment, or pre-label their work.

claim_origin_definitions.explicit: The core proposition, scope, and strength are directly present in the frozen evidence; bounded paraphrase is allowed.
claim_origin_definitions.inferred: A finite traceable combination of supplied evidence supports the claim without external knowledge, causal escalation, scope expansion, or deterministic prediction.
claim_origin_definitions.synthesized: The claim introduces a new abstraction, causal model, prediction, transfer, strategy, scope, or theory not directly stated in the evidence. Synthesized does not automatically mean unsupported.

support_label_definitions.not_assessable: The evidence or claim boundary is insufficient for stable adjudication.
support_label_definitions.partially_supported: The core direction is supported but scope, prediction, certainty, causality, or detail is stronger than the evidence.
support_label_definitions.supported: Claim strength, scope, and causal language are supported by the frozen evidence.
support_label_definitions.unsupported: The material claim cannot be established from the frozen evidence.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: Five percent of payment traffic exposed a connection-pool regression before full migration.
experiences.action_or_observation: Five percent of payment traffic exposed a connection-pool regression before full migration.
experiences.outcome: Only the canary cohort needed rollback; the old version remained available.

experiences.context: Three search cohorts were audited separately during an index rollout.
experiences.action_or_observation: Three search cohorts were audited separately during an index rollout.
experiences.outcome: A language defect was found at cohort two and the remaining tenants were not moved.

experiences.context: Old and new record formats could not coexist during a storage migration.
experiences.action_or_observation: Old and new record formats could not coexist during a storage migration.
experiences.outcome: Partial rollout created incompatible records and more recovery work.

candidate.proposition: In software delivery, deploying changes incrementally to a small subset of traffic enables early detection of defects while keeping the old version available, provided the new and old versions are backward compatible.

predictions.statement: When an incremental rollout with backward compatibility is used for a change, the first cohort will expose any critical regression without affecting the rest of the system.
predictions.observable: A defect found in the first cohort does not require rolling back the old version; only the cohort is rolled back.
predictions.success_criterion: The old version remains fully operational during the entire rollout phase.

falsification_conditions.statement: If an incremental rollout with backward compatibility still causes a system-wide outage or forces rollback of the old version, the pattern is falsified.
falsification_conditions.observable: The entire system becomes unavailable or the old version is rolled back due to issues from the incremental rollout.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In software delivery, deploying changes incrementally to a small subset of traffic enables early detection of defects while keeping the old version available, provided the new and old versions are backward compatible.
When an incremental rollout with backward compatibility is used for a change, the first cohort will expose any critical regression without affecting the rest of the system.
A defect found in the first cohort does not require rolling back the old version; only the cohort is rolled back.
The old version remains fully operational during the entire rollout phase.
If an incremental rollout with backward compatibility still causes a system-wide outage or forces rollback of the old version, the pattern is falsified.
The entire system becomes unavailable or the old version is rolled back due to issues from the incremental rollout.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A support queue labeled cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment.
experiences.action_or_observation: A support queue labeled cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment.
experiences.outcome: Outages reached incident responders quickly while ordinary questions stayed visible.

experiences.context: A billing backlog added severity and regulatory deadline labels.
experiences.action_or_observation: A billing backlog added severity and regulatory deadline labels.
experiences.outcome: Deadline cases stopped being hidden behind newer low-impact tickets.

experiences.context: All VIP messages were ranked above other cases regardless of impact.
experiences.action_or_observation: All VIP messages were ranked above other cases regardless of impact.
experiences.outcome: A widespread login failure waited behind low-impact VIP requests.

candidate.proposition: In customer support queues, labeling cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment ensures that high-impact time-sensitive issues are addressed promptly, while labeling solely by requester status (e.g., VIP) can cause critical issues to be overlooked.

predictions.statement: When a customer support queue labels cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment, high-impact time-sensitive issues will be addressed before lower-impact less-time-sensitive ones.
predictions.observable: The order of case resolution in the queue reflects the combined impact and time-sensitivity labels, with the highest combined score resolved first.
predictions.success_criterion: At least 90% of cases labeled as high-impact and high-time-sensitivity are resolved before any cases labeled as low-impact and low-time-sensitivity in the same time window.

falsification_conditions.statement: If a customer support queue with impact and time-sensitivity labeling experiences a critical outage being delayed behind low-impact VIP requests, the pattern is falsified.
falsification_conditions.observable: A critical outage case receives a lower priority than a low-impact VIP case and is resolved later.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In customer support queues, labeling cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment ensures that high-impact time-sensitive issues are addressed promptly, while labeling solely by requester status (e.g., VIP) can cause critical issues to be overlooked.
When a customer support queue labels cases by customer impact and time sensitivity before assignment, high-impact time-sensitive issues will be addressed before lower-impact less-time-sensitive ones.
The order of case resolution in the queue reflects the combined impact and time-sensitivity labels, with the highest combined score resolved first.
At least 90% of cases labeled as high-impact and high-time-sensitivity are resolved before any cases labeled as low-impact and low-time-sensitivity in the same time window.
If a customer support queue with impact and time-sensitivity labeling experiences a critical outage being delayed behind low-impact VIP requests, the pattern is falsified.
A critical outage case receives a lower priority than a low-impact VIP case and is resolved later.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: An onboarding test fixed activation, sample, guardrail, and stop criteria before launch.
experiences.action_or_observation: An onboarding test fixed activation, sample, guardrail, and stop criteria before launch.
experiences.outcome: The team accepted a neutral result instead of redefining success afterward.

experiences.context: A pricing test fixed revenue lift, cancellation guardrail, and downside budget.
experiences.action_or_observation: A pricing test fixed revenue lift, cancellation guardrail, and downside budget.
experiences.outcome: It stopped when cancellations crossed the guardrail despite temporary revenue gain.

experiences.context: Exploratory interviews used fixed numeric success thresholds before vocabulary was understood.
experiences.action_or_observation: Exploratory interviews used fixed numeric success thresholds before vocabulary was understood.
experiences.outcome: Unexpected themes were discarded even though they later explained customer pain.

candidate.proposition: When a product experiment fixes quantitative success thresholds (activation, stop criteria, guardrails) before launch, the team adheres to those thresholds in outcome evaluation and does not redefine success metrics post-hoc.

predictions.statement: In a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds, the team will accept the outcome based on those thresholds without redefining success.
predictions.observable: The team does not alter success criteria after observing the result.
predictions.success_criterion: The team's final decision (e.g., launch, abandon) matches the predefined threshold triggers.

falsification_conditions.statement: If a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds results in the team redefining success metrics after seeing the outcome, the pattern is falsified.
falsification_conditions.observable: Post-hoc modification of guardrails, stop criteria, or success metrics.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: When a product experiment fixes quantitative success thresholds (activation, stop criteria, guardrails) before launch, the team adheres to those thresholds in outcome evaluation and does not redefine success metrics post-hoc.
In a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds, the team will accept the outcome based on those thresholds without redefining success.
The team does not alter success criteria after observing the result.
The team's final decision (e.g., launch, abandon) matches the predefined threshold triggers.
If a product experiment with predefined quantitative thresholds results in the team redefining success metrics after seeing the outcome, the pattern is falsified.
Post-hoc modification of guardrails, stop criteria, or success metrics.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A cache outage was checked through logs, memory, configuration, permissions, and network evidence.
experiences.action_or_observation: A cache outage was checked through logs, memory, configuration, permissions, and network evidence.
experiences.outcome: A memory limit change was isolated without replacing unrelated components.

experiences.context: A database startup failure used the same evidence classes with service-specific commands.
experiences.action_or_observation: A database startup failure used the same evidence classes with service-specific commands.
experiences.outcome: A filesystem permission change was found before a risky restore.

experiences.context: Routine diagnosis continued while a writer actively corrupted customer records.
experiences.action_or_observation: Routine diagnosis continued while a writer actively corrupted customer records.
experiences.outcome: More records were damaged before writes were isolated.

candidate.proposition: In service_operations, diagnosing a service failure by systematically examining logs, memory, configuration, permissions, and network evidence isolates a specific root cause without replacing unrelated components, provided no active corruption or ongoing damaging writes are present.

applicability_conditions.value: logs, memory, configuration, permissions, network evidence

predictions.statement: For a new service failure in service_operations, applying the same evidence-based diagnosis will isolate a root cause from the examined evidence without needing to replace unrelated components, as long as no active corruption is happening.
predictions.observable: Root cause is identified from logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence; no unnecessary restarts or replacements occur.
predictions.success_criterion: The root cause matches one of the evidence findings and no unrelated components were replaced during recovery.

falsification_conditions.statement: Following the diagnosis process leads to replacing unrelated components or fails to identify a root cause from the evidence, even though no active corruption is present.
falsification_conditions.observable: A service failure is resolved by replacing a component not indicated by logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence, or the root cause is not found within those evidence classes.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In service_operations, diagnosing a service failure by systematically examining logs, memory, configuration, permissions, and network evidence isolates a specific root cause without replacing unrelated components, provided no active corruption or ongoing damaging writes are present.

For a new service failure in service_operations, applying the same evidence-based diagnosis will isolate a root cause from the examined evidence without needing to replace unrelated components, as long as no active corruption is happening.
Root cause is identified from logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence; no unnecessary restarts or replacements occur.
The root cause matches one of the evidence findings and no unrelated components were replaced during recovery.
Following the diagnosis process leads to replacing unrelated components or fails to identify a root cause from the evidence, even though no active corruption is present.
A service failure is resolved by replacing a component not indicated by logs, memory, configuration, permissions, or network evidence, or the root cause is not found within those evidence classes.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: Twenty successful and twenty failed AI posts from the same audience and month were compared.
experiences.action_or_observation: Twenty successful and twenty failed AI posts from the same audience and month were compared.
experiences.outcome: Concrete examples and early problem framing recurred in winners but not controls.

experiences.context: A second account repeated the comparison in another tool category including failed posts.
experiences.action_or_observation: A second account repeated the comparison in another tool category including failed posts.
experiences.outcome: The same features remained associated with saves and qualified comments.

experiences.context: One post went viral only after an unrelated celebrity repost.
experiences.action_or_observation: One post went viral only after an unrelated celebrity repost.
experiences.outcome: Copying its content structure failed on later ordinary posts.

candidate.proposition: In AI tool category posts within the same audience and month, including concrete examples and early problem framing is associated with higher engagement (saves and qualified comments), except when success is driven by an external virality source such as an unrelated celebrity repost.

predictions.statement: If an AI tool category post includes concrete examples and early problem framing, it will have higher saves and qualified comments compared to posts without these features, assuming organic distribution without external virality.
predictions.observable: Number of saves and qualified comments per post within the same audience and month.
predictions.success_criterion: Posts with concrete examples and early problem framing achieve at least 50% more saves and qualified comments than the average of posts without these features in the same audience and month.

falsification_conditions.statement: A post with concrete examples and early problem framing fails to achieve higher engagement, or a post without these features achieves higher engagement due to an identified external virality source (e.g., celebrity repost).
falsification_conditions.observable: Engagement metrics and identification of any external sources driving traffic.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In AI tool category posts within the same audience and month, including concrete examples and early problem framing is associated with higher engagement (saves and qualified comments), except when success is driven by an external virality source such as an unrelated celebrity repost.

If an AI tool category post includes concrete examples and early problem framing, it will have higher saves and qualified comments compared to posts without these features, assuming organic distribution without external virality.
Number of saves and qualified comments per post within the same audience and month.
Posts with concrete examples and early problem framing achieve at least 50% more saves and qualified comments than the average of posts without these features in the same audience and month.

A post with concrete examples and early problem framing fails to achieve higher engagement, or a post without these features achieves higher engagement due to an identified external virality source (e.g., celebrity repost).
Engagement metrics and identification of any external sources driving traffic.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A risky feature was exposed to one internal group behind a reversible flag.
experiences.action_or_observation: A risky feature was exposed to one internal group behind a reversible flag.
experiences.outcome: A dependency assumption failed while reversal still required one configuration change.

experiences.context: One representative tenant was migrated before the full fleet.
experiences.action_or_observation: One representative tenant was migrated before the full fleet.
experiences.outcome: An undocumented data shape was found before fleet-wide commitment.

experiences.context: Only the smallest tenant was tested before generalizing to complex tenants.
experiences.action_or_observation: Only the smallest tenant was tested before generalizing to complex tenants.
experiences.outcome: The full rollout failed on data shapes absent from the probe.

candidate.proposition: In software delivery, exposing a risky change to a representative subset that reflects the complexity of the full population (e.g., one internal group or a representative tenant) reveals hidden issues (like dependency assumption failures or undocumented data shapes) before full rollout, whereas testing only the simplest subset (e.g., only the smallest tenant) fails to detect issues present only in more complex cases.

predictions.statement: If a risky change is first exposed to a representative subset (e.g., a typical tenant or internal group) before full rollout, then at least one hidden issue (dependency assumption failure or undocumented data shape) will be discovered before full rollout.
predictions.observable: Hidden issue is detected during the limited exposure phase and documented before full rollout.
predictions.success_criterion: At least one issue is identified and can be addressed before full rollout.

falsification_conditions.statement: If a change is exposed to a representative subset and no issues are found, but full rollout fails due to issues that were present in that subset's complexity, the pattern is falsified.
falsification_conditions.observable: Full rollout failure due to data shapes or assumptions that existed in the representative subset but were not detected.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In software delivery, exposing a risky change to a representative subset that reflects the complexity of the full population (e.g., one internal group or a representative tenant) reveals hidden issues (like dependency assumption failures or undocumented data shapes) before full rollout, whereas testing only the simplest subset (e.g., only the smallest tenant) fails to detect issues present only in more complex cases.

If a risky change is first exposed to a representative subset (e.g., a typical tenant or internal group) before full rollout, then at least one hidden issue (dependency assumption failure or undocumented data shape) will be discovered before full rollout.
Hidden issue is detected during the limited exposure phase and documented before full rollout.
At least one issue is identified and can be addressed before full rollout.
If a change is exposed to a representative subset and no issues are found, but full rollout fails due to issues that were present in that subset's complexity, the pattern is falsified.
Full rollout failure due to data shapes or assumptions that existed in the representative subset but were not detected.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A rollout declared an error-rate ceiling and automatic pause before traffic increased.
experiences.action_or_observation: A rollout declared an error-rate ceiling and automatic pause before traffic increased.
experiences.outcome: Traffic stopped at twenty percent and prevented a full outage.

experiences.context: An operations change defined queue depth and recovery-time stop conditions.
experiences.action_or_observation: An operations change defined queue depth and recovery-time stop conditions.
experiences.outcome: It reversed before backlog recovery exceeded the overnight window.

experiences.context: A rollout automatically stopped on a noisy metric weakly related to harm.
experiences.action_or_observation: A rollout automatically stopped on a noisy metric weakly related to harm.
experiences.outcome: Repeated false stops delayed delivery without reducing incidents.

candidate.proposition: In incident response, automated rollout stop conditions using directly harmful, low-noise metrics with predefined thresholds prevent full incidents and avoid false stops, whereas noisy metrics weakly related to harm cause repeated false stops and delivery delays without incident reduction.

predictions.statement: If a rollout in incident_response defines an automatic pause based on an error-rate ceiling before traffic increases, then the rollout will stop at a low traffic percentage and prevent a full outage.
predictions.observable: Traffic pauses at or below 20% and no full outage occurs.
predictions.success_criterion: The rollout pauses automatically at the predefined error-rate ceiling, and the system avoids a full outage.

predictions.statement: If an operations change defines automatic stop conditions based on queue depth and recovery-time deadlines, then the change will reverse before backlog recovery exceeds the allowed window.
predictions.observable: The change is reversed before the recovery time exceeds the overnight window.
predictions.success_criterion: The automatic stop triggers and the system recovers within the specified time.

falsification_conditions.statement: If an automated stop condition uses a directly harmful, low-noise metric with a predefined threshold, but the rollout experiences false stops that delay delivery without any incident reduction, then the pattern is falsified.
falsification_conditions.observable: False stops occur and no reduction in incidents is observed, despite the metric being directly harmful and low-noise.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In incident response, automated rollout stop conditions using directly harmful, low-noise metrics with predefined thresholds prevent full incidents and avoid false stops, whereas noisy metrics weakly related to harm cause repeated false stops and delivery delays without incident reduction.
If a rollout in incident_response defines an automatic pause based on an error-rate ceiling before traffic increases, then the rollout will stop at a low traffic percentage and prevent a full outage.
Traffic pauses at or below 20% and no full outage occurs.
The rollout pauses automatically at the predefined error-rate ceiling, and the system avoids a full outage.
If an operations change defines automatic stop conditions based on queue depth and recovery-time deadlines, then the change will reverse before backlog recovery exceeds the allowed window.
The change is reversed before the recovery time exceeds the overnight window.
The automatic stop triggers and the system recovers within the specified time.

If an automated stop condition uses a directly harmful, low-noise metric with a predefined threshold, but the rollout experiences false stops that delay delivery without any incident reduction, then the pattern is falsified.
False stops occur and no reduction in incidents is observed, despite the metric being directly harmful and low-noise.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A reversible lower-risk intervention was used while tests were collected before a high-risk procedure.
experiences.action_or_observation: A reversible lower-risk intervention was used while tests were collected before a high-risk procedure.
experiences.outcome: The condition stabilized and later evidence ruled out the invasive action.

experiences.context: A protocol linked stronger intervention to independent confirmation and worsening signs.
experiences.action_or_observation: A protocol linked stronger intervention to independent confirmation and worsening signs.
experiences.outcome: Escalation remained fast when both confirmation and deterioration appeared.

experiences.context: A team waited for complete evidence despite rapidly worsening vital signs.
experiences.action_or_observation: A team waited for complete evidence despite rapidly worsening vital signs.
experiences.outcome: The delay increased harm because emergency action needed a lower evidence threshold.

candidate.proposition: In clinical safety settings with uncertainty before high-risk procedures, employing a reversible lower-risk intervention while collecting evidence, and linking escalation to both independent confirmation and worsening signs, is associated with stabilization and avoidance of unnecessary invasive action. However, this pattern fails when vital signs are rapidly worsening, requiring immediate action with a lower evidence threshold.

applicability_conditions.value: requires both independent confirmation and worsening signs

predictions.statement: If a reversible lower-risk intervention is used while tests are pending before a high-risk procedure, and escalation is conditioned on both independent confirmation and worsening signs, then the patient's condition will stabilize and invasive action will be avoided.
predictions.observable: Patient condition stabilizes and later evidence rules out the need for the initially considered invasive action.
predictions.success_criterion: Outcome matches the observed outcome in memory px_08_support_1.

falsification_conditions.statement: If vital signs are rapidly worsening, applying the staged approach with reversible lower-risk intervention and conditioned escalation results in increased harm due to delay.
falsification_conditions.observable: Delay in emergency action leads to measurable worsening of patient outcome, consistent with counterexample px_08_counter_3.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In clinical safety settings with uncertainty before high-risk procedures, employing a reversible lower-risk intervention while collecting evidence, and linking escalation to both independent confirmation and worsening signs, is associated with stabilization and avoidance of unnecessary invasive action. However, this pattern fails when vital signs are rapidly worsening, requiring immediate action with a lower evidence threshold.

If a reversible lower-risk intervention is used while tests are pending before a high-risk procedure, and escalation is conditioned on both independent confirmation and worsening signs, then the patient's condition will stabilize and invasive action will be avoided.
Patient condition stabilizes and later evidence rules out the need for the initially considered invasive action.
Outcome matches the observed outcome in memory px_08_support_1.
If vital signs are rapidly worsening, applying the staged approach with reversible lower-risk intervention and conditioned escalation results in increased harm due to delay.
Delay in emergency action leads to measurable worsening of patient outcome, consistent with counterexample px_08_counter_3.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: Two laboratories used different instruments and samples to estimate one material property.
experiences.action_or_observation: Two laboratories used different instruments and samples to estimate one material property.
experiences.outcome: Their estimates converged within a preregistered tolerance.

experiences.context: Observation, transaction records, and independently collected interviews tested one claim.
experiences.action_or_observation: Observation, transaction records, and independently collected interviews tested one claim.
experiences.outcome: All three sources supported the same behavioral change.

experiences.context: Five reports repeated a number originating from one unverified blog post.
experiences.action_or_observation: Five reports repeated a number originating from one unverified blog post.
experiences.outcome: The apparent consensus disappeared when the source was corrected.

candidate.proposition: When independent sources (different instruments, methods, or data collection processes) converge on the same estimate or conclusion, the result is robust. In contrast, apparent consensus from multiple reports that merely repeat a single unverified source is fragile and collapses when the original source is corrected.

predictions.statement: If two independent laboratories measure the same material property using different instruments and samples, their estimates will converge within a preregistered tolerance.
predictions.observable: The difference between the two estimates is within the tolerance range.

falsification_conditions.statement: If two independent laboratories measure the same property and their estimates do not converge within the preregistered tolerance, the pattern fails.
falsification_conditions.observable: The difference between estimates exceeds the tolerance.

falsification_conditions.statement: If multiple reports supporting a claim are all traced to a single unverified original source, the pattern of apparent consensus does not hold.
falsification_conditions.observable: Correction of the original source causes the consensus to disappear.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: When independent sources (different instruments, methods, or data collection processes) converge on the same estimate or conclusion, the result is robust. In contrast, apparent consensus from multiple reports that merely repeat a single unverified source is fragile and collapses when the original source is corrected.
If two independent laboratories measure the same material property using different instruments and samples, their estimates will converge within a preregistered tolerance.
The difference between the two estimates is within the tolerance range.
abs(estimate_1 - estimate_2) <= tolerance
If two independent laboratories measure the same property and their estimates do not converge within the preregistered tolerance, the pattern fails.
The difference between estimates exceeds the tolerance.
If multiple reports supporting a claim are all traced to a single unverified original source, the pattern of apparent consensus does not hold.
Correction of the original source causes the consensus to disappear.

evidence_input.objective: Extract one bounded, evidence-grounded Pattern Candidate from the supplied experiences.

experiences.context: A manufacturer buffered a component with twelve-week replenishment and volatile demand.
experiences.action_or_observation: A manufacturer buffered a component with twelve-week replenishment and volatile demand.
experiences.outcome: Interruptions fell while carrying cost stayed within its declared limit.

experiences.context: A distributor buffered only products with predictable spikes and slow supplier response.
experiences.action_or_observation: A distributor buffered only products with predictable spikes and slow supplier response.
experiences.outcome: Stockouts fell during peak demand and inventory returned to baseline later.

experiences.context: The same large buffer was held for an item replenished daily with stable demand.
experiences.action_or_observation: The same large buffer was held for an item replenished daily with stable demand.
experiences.outcome: Most inventory expired and cost exceeded avoided stockouts.

candidate.proposition: In supply chains, buffering inventory reduces interruptions or stockouts when replenishment lead time is long and demand is volatile, or when demand has predictable spikes and supplier response is slow. Buffering is counterproductive when replenishment lead time is short and demand is stable.

predictions.statement: For products with long replenishment lead time and volatile demand, inventory buffering reduces interruptions while keeping carrying cost within the declared limit.
predictions.observable: Interruption events decrease and carrying cost as a percentage of product value does not exceed the planned budget.
predictions.success_criterion: Measured over a typical planning horizon, stockout frequency drops by at least 30% and carrying cost remains under the target threshold set by management.

falsification_conditions.statement: For a product with daily replenishment and stable demand, applying the same large buffer leads to inventory expiration and total cost exceeding the value of avoided stockouts.
falsification_conditions.observable: Observed inventory write-offs due to expiration and total holding cost greater than the cost of stockouts that would have occurred without buffering.

claim_source_anchors.source_text: In supply chains, buffering inventory reduces interruptions or stockouts when replenishment lead time is long and demand is volatile, or when demand has predictable spikes and supplier response is slow. Buffering is counterproductive when replenishment lead time is short and demand is stable.
For products with long replenishment lead time and volatile demand, inventory buffering reduces interruptions while keeping carrying cost within the declared limit.
Interruption events decrease and carrying cost as a percentage of product value does not exceed the planned budget.
Measured over a typical planning horizon, stockout frequency drops by at least 30% and carrying cost remains under the target threshold set by management.
For a product with daily replenishment and stable demand, applying the same large buffer leads to inventory expiration and total cost exceeding the value of avoided stockouts.
Observed inventory write-offs due to expiration and total holding cost greater than the cost of stockouts that would have occurred without buffering.
